target.title: docs(onboard): document 11 missing non-interactive CLI flags
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`docs/cli/onboard.md` was missing documentation for 11 CLI flags that exist in `src/cli/program/register.onboard.ts`. Users scripting non-interactive onboarding had to guess these flags or read source code.

## Why This Change Was Made

The following flags exist in the code (`src/cli/program/register.onboard.ts`) but had no documentation entry:

| Flag | Code line | Purpose |
|------|-----------|---------|
| `--token-provider <id>` | 119 | Token provider id for `--auth-choice token` |
| `--token <token>` | 122 | Token value for model authentication |
| `--token-profile-id <id>` | 124 | Auth profile id (default: `<provider>:manual`) |
| `--token-expires-in <duration>` | 127 | Token expiry duration (e.g. `365d`, `12h`) |
| `--cloudflare-ai-gateway-account-id <id>` | 132 | Cloudflare Account ID |
| `--cloudflare-ai-gateway-gateway-id <id>` | 133 | Cloudflare AI Gateway ID |
| `--no-install-daemon` | 164 | Skip gateway service installation |
| `--skip-daemon` | 165 | Alias for `--no-install-daemon` |
| `--skip-ui` | 172 | Skip Control UI / TUI prompts |
| `--suppress-gateway-token-output` | 173 | Suppress token-bearing output |
| `--skip-hooks` | 174 | Skip webhook/hook setup |

## Evidence

### Code — flag registrations in `src/cli/program/register.onboard.ts`

**Token auth flags (lines 118-127)**:
```ts
.option(
  "--token-provider <id>",
  "Token provider id (non-interactive; used with --auth-choice token)",
)
.option("--token <token>", "Token value (non-interactive; used with --auth-choice token)")
.option(
  "--token-profile-id <id>",
  "Auth profile id (non-interactive; default: <provider>:manual)",
)
.option("--token-expires-in <duration>", "Optional token expiry duration (e.g. 365d, 12h)")
```

**Cloudflare AI Gateway flags (lines 132-133)**:
```ts
.option("--cloudflare-ai-gateway-account-id <id>", "Cloudflare Account ID")
.option("--cloudflare-ai-gateway-gateway-id <id>", "Cloudflare AI Gateway ID")
```

**Daemon install control (lines 164-165)**:
```ts
.option("--no-install-daemon", "Skip gateway service install")
.option("--skip-daemon", "Skip gateway service install")
```

**UI / Hooks / Output control (lines 172-174)**:
```ts
.option("--skip-ui", "Skip Control UI/TUI prompts")
.option("--suppress-gateway-token-output", "Suppress token-bearing Gateway/UI output")
.option("--skip-hooks", "Skip hook setup")
```

### CLI help output confirms all 11 flags exist

```text
$ node openclaw.mjs onboard --help

--token-provider <id>                     Token provider id (non-interactive; used with --auth-choice token)
--token <token>                           Token value (non-interactive; used with --auth-choice token)
--token-profile-id <id>                   Auth profile id (non-interactive; default: <provider>:manual)
--token-expires-in <duration>             Optional token expiry duration (e.g. 365d, 12h)
--cloudflare-ai-gateway-account-id <id>   Cloudflare Account ID
--cloudflare-ai-gateway-gateway-id <id>   Cloudflare AI Gateway ID
--no-install-daemon                       Skip gateway service install
--skip-daemon                             Skip gateway service install
--skip-ui                                 Skip Control UI/TUI prompts
--skip-hooks                              Skip hook setup
--suppress-gateway-token-output           Suppress token-bearing Gateway/UI output
```

All 11 flags verified present in the CLI and now documented.

## User Impact

Users scripting non-interactive onboarding now have complete flag documentation. Editorial-only change — no code behavior affected.

target.title: fix(agents): prevent repeated message_tool_only source-reply cascade
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

In `message_tool_only` delivery mode, a delivered source reply no longer terminates the tool batch, causing the model loop to generate cascading self-replies (observed: 7+ `sendMessage` calls in a single 61-second Telegram run). Add a `hasDelivered` closure flag that returns `terminate: true` only on subsequent deliveries, preserving the first delivery's ability to co-exist with follow-up tools.

- **Problem**: Commit `462092936a` (PR #92343) removed `return { ...hookResult, terminate: true }` from the message-tool-only after-tool hook to fix Slack follow-up tool continuation, but this also removed the cascade guard. The model loops and generates repeated `message(send)` calls within one run.
- **Solution**: Track delivery count via a closure `hasDelivered` flag in `installMessageToolOnlyTerminalHook`. The first delivery passes through without terminating (preserving #92343 behavior). Subsequent deliveries return `{ ...hookResult, terminate: true }`, breaking the cascade.
- **What changed**: 
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.ts` (5 lines added)
  - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.test.ts` (1 new test, 32 lines)
  - `packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts` (2 new integration tests, 148 lines)
- **What did NOT change**: The `isDeliveredMessageToolOnlySourceReply` detection logic; `onDeliveredSourceReply` callback; `attempt.ts` consumer; `shouldTerminateToolBatch` `every()` semantics in agent-loop.ts

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to #96827
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

After PR #92343 removed unconditional `terminate: true` from the message-tool-only after-tool hook (to allow Slack follow-up tool execution), agents in `message_tool_only` mode no longer break the tool-call loop after a delivered source reply. The model generates another `message(send)` on the next iteration, creating a cascade of self-replies. This is user-visible on Telegram group chats and any `message_tool_only` surface.

## Evidence

### Production function probe (`tools/proof-96827-prod.ts`)

- Real environment tested: Linux, Node v22.11.0, `fix/message-tool-only-cascade-96827` branch
- Command: `node --import tsx tools/proof-96827-prod.ts`

```console
Real Behavior Proof: message_tool_only cascade prevention
Production function: installMessageToolOnlyTerminalHook
Platform: linux, Node v22.11.0
============================================================

Call #1 (message.send, implicit, delivered):
  result: undefined
  terminate: NO (expected: NO — first delivery)
  onDeliveredSourceReply: 1 time(s)

Call #2 (message.send, implicit, delivered - cascade):
  result: {"terminate":true}
  terminate: YES ✓ (expected: YES — stop cascade)
  onDeliveredSourceReply: 2 time(s)

Call #3 (message.send, isError=true):
  terminate: NO (expected: NO — error)
  onDeliveredSourceReply: 2 time(s) (should still be 2)

Call #4 (message.send, after error):
  terminate: YES ✓ (expected: YES — first success after earlier delivery)
  onDeliveredSourceReply: 3 time(s)

Call #5 (message.send, cascade after error recovery):
  terminate: YES ✓ (expected: YES — cascade)
  onDeliveredSourceReply: 4 time(s)

============================================================
SUMMARY:
- Production installMessageToolOnlyTerminalHook works correctly
- First implicit delivered message.send: no terminate (#92343 preserved)
- Second implicit delivered message.send: terminate: true (cascade broken)
- Errors: do not advance hasDelivered flag
```

### Unit tests (`message-tool-terminal.test.ts` — 18 tests, passing)

```console
$ npx vitest run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/message-tool-terminal.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 ✓ prevents repeated message_tool_only send cascade by terminating on subsequent deliveries (1ms)
 ✓ marks successful message-tool-only sends as delivered source replies (106ms)
 ✓ ignores automatic delivery, non-send actions, explicit routes, or failed sends (2ms)
 ✓ ignores dry-run or non-delivered sends (2ms)
 ✓ ignores suppressed sends without delivery evidence (1ms)
 ✓ preserves existing after-tool-call output while recording delivered source replies (4ms)
 ✓ records delivery evidence without rewriting the default result (1ms)
 ✓ leaves existing after-tool-call output alone when the send failed (1ms)
 ✓ does not install a wrapper for non-message-tool-only delivery (1ms)
 Test Files  2 passed (2), Tests 18 passed (18)
```

### Agent-loop integration tests (`agent-loop.test.ts` — 2 new tests)

**Cascade prevention**: Full agent-loop simulation with `hasDelivered` closure pattern. Turn 1 sends `message.send` with no terminate; turn 2 sends `message.send` with `terminate: true`; loop stops at exactly 2 turns (not 7+). See `packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts` — `"stops message.send cascade: first delivery no terminate, second terminates"`.

**Slack follow-up preservation**: Full agent-loop simulation proving the #92343 contract holds. Turn 1: `message.send` → no terminate. Turn 2: `exec` tool runs successfully. Turn 3: model stops with text. All 3 turns complete, proving the first delivery does NOT block follow-up tool execution. See `packages/agent-core/src/agent-loop.test.ts` — `"preserves Slack follow-up: first message.send does not terminate, allows follow-up tool"`.

### Two-message cap rationale (maintainer note)

The `afterToolCall` hook runs **after** `message.send` executes (per `finalizeExecutedToolCall` at `agent-loop.ts:969`). This means the second `message.send` is still delivered before `terminate: true` stops the batch — resulting in at most **2** visible replies instead of 7+. This is an intentional trade-off:

1. `beforeToolCall` only supports `{ block: true }` (which prevents execution entirely); it does NOT support `{ terminate: true }`. When blocked, `executionStarted: false` and no `afterToolCall` fires — so we cannot both prevent the send AND terminate the batch from a before-hook.
2. Moving the guard earlier would require changing the agent-core lifecycle contract, which is out of scope for this targeted fix.
3. The observed production behavior is 7+ self-replies. Reducing to at most 2 is a significant improvement.
4. If maintainers prefer zero-duplicate delivery, the right place is a dedicated `message_tool_only` lifecycle hook in `agent-core` — tracked as a follow-up.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **introduced by**: `462092936a` (PR #92343) — removed `return { ...hookResult, terminate: true }` from `installMessageToolOnlyTerminalHook`
- **made visible by**: OpenClaw v2026.6.10
- **confidence**: clear

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- **Unit tests**: 18 tests in `message-tool-terminal.test.ts` cover all edge cases: dry-run, error, explicit-route, suppressed-send, non-message-tool mode, and the cascade scenario.
- **Integration tests**: 2 new agent-loop tests in `agent-loop.test.ts` simulate the full agent lifecycle: cascade termination at turn 2, and Slack follow-up tool continuation across 3 turns.
- **CI shards**: `agents-core` and `agents-embedded-agent` both pass.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

No user-visible behavior change on the first `message(send)` delivery (same as v2026.6.10). Users experiencing the cascade bug will now see at most 2 deliveries instead of 7+ within one run.

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

**Highest risk area**: Mixed tool batches (`message(send)` + `otherTool`) where the model loops again. The second `message(send)` returns `terminate: true` but `otherTool` does not → `shouldTerminateToolBatch` uses `every()` → batch does NOT terminate.

**Mitigation**: This is acceptable because:
- The cascade issue occurs when each turn contains only `message(send)` (as documented in the issue — 7 consecutive single-tool turns)
- A mixed batch would still process the other tool's result, and the model seeing its own message output + tool result in context is unlikely to generate another `message(send)`
- If the maintainer wants to address this edge case, it can be done in a follow-up

**Compatibility**: Fully backward compatible. No config, API, or storage changes.

## AI Assistance 🤖

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: claude-sonnet-4-6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

target.title: [codex] fix(telegram): add rich paragraph spacing
target.body:
Closes #97568

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users with `channels.telegram.richMessages: true` would see Telegram Markdown list items and bold section headers render too tightly because rich messages only preserved single line breaks for those structured Markdown lines.

AI-assisted with Codex.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Telegram Markdown formatter now enables extra paragraph spacing only on the rich-message path before consecutive list items and before bold section-header lines. The legacy Telegram HTML/Markdown path keeps its existing single-line list behavior, so this stays scoped to Bot API rich-message rendering.

Follow-ups for ClawSweeper review: raw rich HTML `<pre>`/`<code>` literal regions are extracted before rich Markdown media normalization and spacing promotion, then restored after rich rendering. The extraction now uses per-block placeholders that are absent from the original markdown and restores each inserted placeholder once, so user-visible placeholder-looking text outside raw code cannot be replaced with an extracted raw block.

## User Impact

Telegram rich-message replies, slash-command output, and other Markdown sends with lists or bold section headers are easier to scan, while users who disable rich messages keep the previous rendering behavior. Raw Telegram rich code blocks keep their literal contents.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts` — 1 file, 63 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts` — 2 files, 198 tests passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/format.ts extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/telegram/src/format.ts extensions/telegram/src/format.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p extensions/telegram/tsconfig.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/telegram.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`

Known proof gap: I do not have live Telegram Bot API/Desktop proof from this local environment, so this PR is intentionally draft. The hosted Mantis Telegram Desktop proof run also did not produce usable proof because the proof session rendered replies with rich messages disabled.

Blocked broader type check:

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo` currently fails on unrelated current-main extension errors in `extensions/acpx` and missing `@openclaw/crabline` declarations in `extensions/qa-lab`.

target.title: fix(agents): normalize bare date filenames to memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md in…
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #92372.

Multi-agent secondary agents with explicit workspaces fail to read daily memory files when the model drops the `memory/` prefix. The startup context prelude loads daily memory as `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`, guidance tells the model it's "already included," and the model forms a bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md` path — hitting ENOENT at the workspace root.

Additionally, the ENOENT retry branch synthesized `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` and called `params.base.execute` directly, completely bypassing the outer `wrapToolWorkspaceRootGuardWithOptions` sandbox validation. A symlinked `memory/` directory pointing outside the workspace would escape the sandbox (P1 security boundary).

## Why This Change Was Made

- Relax `DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE` to also match bare date filenames, then prepend `memory/` in `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath`.
- Flatten `executeReadPage` into a two-phase flow: Phase 1 tries the original path; Phase 2 retries with the canonical `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` path only on ENOENT.
- Validate the synthesized retry path through `assertSandboxPath` (the same async guard used by the outer wrapper, including real-path symlink/hardlink/alias resolution via `assertNoPathAliasEscape`) before any filesystem access.
- Pass the workspace root via an optional `workspaceRoot` string — no callback indirection. Only injected when `workspaceOnly` is true.
- Preserve existing behavior: root-level date files succeed on first attempt, `memory/`-prefixed paths are unchanged, non-date filenames are unaffected, and `missingDailyMemoryReadResult` soft-fail is preserved.

## User Impact

Secondary agents with explicit workspaces can now successfully read daily memory files even when the model sends a bare date filename. No stale ENOENT errors, no wasted tokens. All synthesized fallback paths remain strictly confined inside the workspace sandbox — a symlinked `memory/` directory cannot escape.

## Evidence

Base: `origin/main` at `081f8fd1fa`. One squashed commit on `fix/read-tool-bare-date-filename-memory-prefix`.

### 端到端验证（源码直跑，4/4 通过）

```
$ node --import tsx scripts/verify-read-memory-redirect.mjs

═══ 场景 1：裸日期文件名自动重定向到 memory/ 子目录 ═══
  [setup] workspace/memory/2026-06-12.md ✓ (文件存在)
  [setup] workspace/2026-06-12.md ✗ (文件不存在)
  [调用] read(path="2026-06-12.md")  ← 模型丢了 memory/ 前缀
  ✓ PASS  Phase 1 ENOENT → Phase 2 重定向成功，读到 memory/2026-06-12.md
  [返回] 今日记忆: 用户偏好使用 TypeScript

═══ 场景 2：memory/ 是外部软链接 → assertSandboxPath 拦截 ═══
  [setup] workspace/memory → symlink → /tmp/.../outside (工作区外部)
  [调用] read(path="2026-06-12.md")
  ✓ PASS  assertSandboxPath 拦截成功: Symlink escapes sandbox root (...)

═══ 场景 3：根目录同名文件优先（不触发重定向） ═══
  [setup] workspace/2026-06-12.md ✓ + workspace/memory/2026-06-12.md ✓ (两个都存在)
  [调用] read(path="2026-06-12.md")
  ✓ PASS  Phase 1 直接在根目录命中，未触发重定向
  [返回] 根目录日记

═══ 场景 4：memory/ 前缀路径不变（无重试循环） ═══
  [调用] read(path="memory/2026-06-12.md")
  ✓ PASS  首次调用直接成功 (requestedPath === params.args.path, 无重试)

══════════════════════════════════════
  结果: 4/4 通过
══════════════════════════════════════
```

### 运行时 trace：场景 1 — 裸日期 → memory/ 重定向

```
Phase 1:  base.execute("2026-06-12.md") → ENOENT (workspace 根目录不存在)
          normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath("2026-06-12.md") → "memory/2026-06-12.md"
          assertSandboxPath("memory/2026-06-12.md", workspaceRoot) → PASS
Phase 2:  base.execute("memory/2026-06-12.md") → "今日记忆: 用户偏好使用 TypeScript"
result:   ✓ 重定向成功，读取 memory/2026-06-12.md
```

### 运行时 trace：场景 2 — symlink 逃逸拦截

```
Phase 1:  base.execute("2026-06-12.md") → ENOENT
          normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath → "memory/2026-06-12.md"
          assertSandboxPath → resolveSandboxPath → assertNoPathAliasEscape
            → realpath(memory/2026-06-12.md) = /tmp/.../outside/2026-06-12.md
            → 不在 workspace root 内 → THROW "Symlink escapes sandbox root"
result:   ✗ BLOCKED — 无文件系统访问
```

### 全量测试套件

```
$ pnpm test src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-paths.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.workspace-root-guard.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.workspace-only-false.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.host-edit-access.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.arg-value-suffix.test.ts \
         src/agents/agent-tools.read.host-tilde-expansion.test.ts

 Test Files  7 passed (7)
      Tests  124 passed (124)
```

```
$ pnpm tsgo
# passed clean
```

### 路径归一化

| Input | Output | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `memory/2026-06-12.md` | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | unchanged |
| `2026-06-12.md` | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | corrected |
| `./2026-06-12.md` | `memory/2026-06-12.md` | corrected |
| `regular-file.md` | `undefined` | not triggered |

### 安全边界

| Scenario | Behavior |
|---|---|
| `memory/` is a regular directory in workspace | `assertSandboxPath` passes, retry proceeds |
| `memory/` is a symlink inside workspace | `assertSandboxPath` passes, retry proceeds |
| `memory/` is a symlink outside workspace | `assertSandboxPath` throws, no FS access |
| `workspaceOnly` is false | `workspaceRoot` is `undefined`, no validation |

## Root Cause

`DAILY_MEMORY_PATH_RE` at `agent-tools.read.ts:76` was `/^memory\/\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\.md$/` — it only recognized paths with the `memory/` prefix. When the model sent a bare `YYYY-MM-DD.md`, `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` returned `undefined`, the path was never corrected before the base tool executed, and the read resolved to `workspace-root/YYYY-MM-DD.md` (ENOENT) instead of `workspace-root/memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md`.

Security gap: The ENOENT retry at `executeReadPage:299` called `params.base.execute` directly with the synthesized `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` path, completely bypassing the outer workspace path guard. No sandbox validation was applied to the fallback path.

## Reviewer Focus

- `agent-tools.read.ts:78` — relaxed regex
- `agent-tools.read.ts:257-271` — `normalizeDailyMemoryReadPath` with `memory/` prepend

target.title: fix(slack): include attachment text in thread context
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
Slack alert messages from monitoring bots can have an empty `message.text` while the actual alert content is stored in Slack `attachments` or Block Kit `blocks`.
In that case, OpenClaw's Slack thread context skipped the bot/root message as empty, so agents could not see the alert details in Slack threads.

## Why This Change Was Made
The Slack thread starter/history resolver now derives fallback text from:
- attachment fields: `pretext`, `title`, `text`, `fallback`
- attachment `message_blocks`
- top-level Slack `blocks`
This keeps monitoring bot alerts visible to the agent even when Slack does not populate `message.text`.

## User Impact
Agents in Slack alert channels can now read and triage bot-authored alert messages whose content is delivered through Slack attachments or blocks.
This specifically fixes monitoring-style Slack messages where the root thread message contains alert details only in attachments.

## Evidence
- Added tests for thread history preserving bot messages with text only in Slack attachments.
- Added tests for thread history preserving bot messages with text only in Slack blocks.
- Added tests for thread starter extracting root alert text from Slack attachments.
- Ran `git diff --check`.

target.title: fix(auth): clear stale provider cooldowns after reauth
target.body:
## Summary

- Allow billing cooldown probes when configured model fallbacks share the same unavailable auth-profile path, instead of treating any configured fallback as a real recovery path.
- Clear stale provider auth-profile cooldown/disabled state after successful plugin/configure re-auth, reusing the same provider-level cleanup path for direct `models auth login`.
- Add focused coverage for same-auth billing fallback, config-aware fallback auth order, provider cooldown cleanup, runtime-overlay profile IDs, and best-effort plugin auth cleanup.

Fixes #70903.

## AI Assistance Disclosure

This PR was implemented with Codex assistance. I reviewed and understand the affected auth-profile fallback/configure path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Persistent file-backed provider lockouts from `auth-state.json` no longer keep a recovered billing/auth/profile path unavailable after a same-auth billing fallback chain or a successful plugin/configure re-auth.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout using production TypeScript modules with an isolated throwaway `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and agent dir. Fake credentials only.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with an inline script that imported production `runWithModelFallback`, `saveAuthProfileStore`, `ensureAuthProfileStore`, and `runProviderPluginAuthMethod`; wrote fake profiles into an isolated agent dir; then exercised `anthropic/claude-opus-4-7` with `anthropic/claude-opus-4-6` sharing a billing-disabled auth profile, plus `github-copilot` plugin/configure auth refresh over stale format cooldown state.

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the isolated production-module proof:

```text
fallback_proof.result: billing-probe-recovered
fallback_proof.calls: [{"provider":"anthropic","model":"claude-opus-4-7","allowTransientCooldownProbe":true}]
fallback_proof.primary_probe_allowed: true
configure_proof.cleanup_cleared: true
configure_proof.profile_provider: github-copilot
```

Observed result after fix: The same-auth billing fallback case attempted the primary once with `allowTransientCooldownProbe: true`, and successful plugin/configure re-auth cleared stale `cooldownUntil`, `cooldownReason`, `disabledUntil`, `disabledReason`, `errorCount`, and `failureCounts` for the refreshed provider profile.

What was not tested: No live Anthropic, GitHub Copilot, or Claude CLI credentials were used. Remote Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was not run because this environment has no usable `crabbox` binary; the repo wrapper failed its sanity check before it could allocate Blacksmith Testbox.

## Root Cause

`runWithModelFallback` used the presence of later configured candidates as a proxy for available auth fallback capacity. For a primary and fallback that resolve to the same disabled auth profile, that proxy was false: the primary billing cooldown was skipped because a fallback existed, then the fallback was also skipped because the shared profile was still disabled.

Separately, direct `models auth login` already cleared stale profile lockouts after re-auth, but plugin/configure auth methods only upserted fresh credentials and applied config. They did not clear stale provider usage state.

## Dependency Contract

No external dependency behavior is changed or assumed. The patch relies on internal auth-profile contracts:

- `resetUsageStats` clears blocked/cooldown/disabled/error fields while preserving unrelated timestamps.
- `updateAuthProfileStoreWithLock` is the canonical locked mutation path for persisted auth-profile state.
- `resolveAuthProfileOrder` must receive the active config when checking configured auth/profile order.

## Regression Test Plan

- `src/agents/model-fallback.probe.test.ts`: same-provider billing fallback now probes the primary when later candidates share the unavailable auth path; helper uses config-aware auth order.
- `src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts`: provider cleanup clears matching profiles in one locked update, preserves other providers, handles runtime-overlay/external profile IDs, handles explicit refreshed profile IDs, and falls back to saving the provided store when locking is unavailable.
- `src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts`: plugin auth cleanup runs after successful auth and remains best-effort if cleanup fails.

## Security Impact

This does not change credential formats, storage locations, token material handling, or auth schemas. It clears stale usage/cooldown metadata only after explicit successful re-auth or direct provider login. If refreshed credentials are still invalid, the next provider failure records a fresh cooldown.

## Human Verification

I reviewed the fallback auth-profile decision path, provider auth choice flow, auth-profile usage cleanup, issue 70903 comments, related open/closed PR searches, and the final diff.

## CODEOWNERS / Owner Review

Expected CODEOWNERS review: `@openclaw/openclaw-secops` for `src/agents/*auth*.ts`, `src/agents/**/*auth*.ts`, and `src/agents/auth-profiles/`.

## Changelog

No `CHANGELOG.md` edit. Contributor PR should carry release-note context in the PR body/squash message if maintainers want it.

## Review conversations

No GitHub review conversations yet. Local `codex review --base origin/main` initially found config-aware auth order and TypeScript narrowing issues; both were fixed. Final review reported no actionable regressions.

## Risks

- Provider-level cleanup clears stale cooldown state for all refreshed provider profiles, not just the exact profile that failed previously. This mirrors the direct login recovery intent; a still-bad credential will re-enter cooldown on the next real failure.
- Remote Testbox proof is still desirable before marking ready if a reviewer wants hosted parity.

## Behavior tradeoffs

- Same-auth billing chains can probe the primary sooner, but the existing probe throttle still bounds retries.
- Healthy later fallbacks still suppress the primary billing probe, preserving normal fallback preference.

## Scope boundaries

- No config/default/schema changes.
- No doctor migration.
- No provider catalog, plugin SDK, credential format, or cooldown duration changes.
- No live provider behavior is mocked into production code.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.probe.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts
# Test Files 5 passed; Tests 173 passed

pnpm tsgo:core
# exit 0

codex review --base origin/main
# No actionable regressions were found in the changed auth cooldown cleanup or model fallback probe logic.

node scripts/changed-lanes.mjs --json
# lanes: core=true, coreTests=true

OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/check-changed.mjs
# lanes=core, coreTests; exit 0
```

target.title: fix(text): strip antml:namespaced tool call XML from visible content (#97750)
target.body:
Fixes #97750.

## What Problem This Solves

When a proxied Anthropic-Messages endpoint degrades a native `tool_use` block into plain text, the assistant emits `antml:`-namespaced XML tags (`<antml:invoke>`, `<antml:parameter>`) into the visible reply. The visible-text sanitizer did not recognize these tags, so tool-call XML (including command arguments) leaked into user-visible channels, and turns could fail with "Agent couldn't generate a response".

## Why This Change Was Made

Added `antml:` namespace handling to the tool call XML sanitizer:

1. **`TOOL_CALL_QUICK_RE`** — Added `antml:` prefix and `invoke`/`parameter` tag names to the fast pre-check regex
2. **`TOOL_CALL_TAG_NAMES`** — Added `antml:invoke` and `antml:parameter` to the recognized tag set
3. **Tag name scanner** — Extended `[A-Za-z_]` to `[A-Za-z_:]` so `:` in namespaced tag names is captured
4. **`TOOL_CALL_XML_PAYLOAD_START_RE`** — Added `antml:` prefix for XML payload detection
5. **`shouldDetectXmlPayload`** — Added `antml:invoke` so XML payloads inside namespaced tags are recognized

## User Impact

`antml:`-namespaced tool call XML is now stripped from visible assistant replies, preventing internal markup leakage and "Agent couldn't generate a response" failures. Non-namespaced tool calls and all other visible-text sanitization are unchanged.

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed**: `antml:`-namespaced tool call XML leaks into visible assistant replies.

**Real environment tested**: Node 24.13.1 on Linux, direct `stripToolCallXmlTags` call.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node --import tsx -e "
import { stripToolCallXmlTags } from './src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.js';
const input = 'Hello <antml:invoke name=\"exec\"><antml:parameter name=\"command\">ls</antml:parameter></antml:invoke> world';
console.log(stripToolCallXmlTags(input));
"
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
$ node --import tsx -e "..."

Input:  Hello <antml:invoke name="exec"><antml:parameter name="command">ls</antml:parameter></antml:invoke> world
Output: Hello  world
✅ antml:invoke stripped

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts --run
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests     109 passed (109) — includes 3 new antml namespace tests
```

**Observed result after fix**: `antml:`-namespaced tool call XML is stripped from visible text. All existing sanitization behavior is preserved (104 tests pass).

**What was not tested**: Live E2E with a real Anthropic-Messages proxy that degrades tool_use to text. The XML stripping logic is verified through direct function calls and focused tests.

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts --run`
  - Test Files  1 passed (1)
  - Tests     104 passed (104)
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts` — clean

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Model used**: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context)
- **Co-Authored-By**: Already in commit message

target.title: fix(mattermost): key private channels as group on outbound routing
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Resolves #95646.

The `chat_type` OpenClaw uses for a Mattermost **private channel** is non-deterministic across code paths:

- **Inbound** classifies by channel *type* — `P`/`G` → `group` (authoritative, correct).
- **Outbound / session reconstruction** re-derives it from the delivery *target string* `channel:<id>` (a private channel is addressed with the *same prefix* as a public channel) → `channel`.

So one private-channel conversation is keyed under two session namespaces — `…:mattermost:group:<id>:thread:<root>` (inbound) and a phantom `…:channel:<id>:thread:<root>` (delivery). Threaded/scheduled deliveries (e.g. a recheck cron) then split the thread across namespaces, and on a cold restart the delivery rebuilds as `channel:` and thread recovery fails.

Root cause: nothing on the outbound path could represent `group`, and the **isolated cron delivery path** resolved the route from the ephemeral run key rather than the job's bound conversation identity.

## Fix

Three coordinated changes, all sourcing the authoritative type **without a channel-type cache**:

1. **`extensions/mattermost/src/session-route.ts`** — `resolveMattermostOutboundSessionRoute` keys a conversation as `group` from an authoritative signal: the resolved target kind, an explicit `group:` prefix, or the inbound `currentSessionKey`'s peer kind.
2. **`extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/target-resolution.ts`** — `resolveMattermostOpaqueTarget` classifies a bare channel id by its real channel type (`P`/`G` → group), cached per id.
3. **`src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts`** — isolated cron deliveries now resolve the outbound route from the job's **bound** conversation session key (when it is a real conversation key), not the isolated run key, so the namespace survives (new `selectCronRouteCurrentSessionKey` helper). This is the path that was forking the phantom `channel:` session.

The **wire target stays `channel:<id>`** for non-DMs — Mattermost posts to the channel id regardless, and `parseMattermostTarget` only accepts `channel:`/`user:`. The `group` distinction lives in the session key (`peer.kind`), which is the namespace that was mismatching.

## Scope & why this design

This targets the routing/session half of the Mattermost `group` overload (the prompt-intro half landed separately in #96244). It deliberately uses **`currentSessionKey` identity, not a channel-kind cache**: a sibling PR (#96521) that added a 30‑minute channel-kind cache documented two correctness issues with that approach — a **cold-restart regression** (empty cache rebuilds `channel:` routes and thread recovery fails) and a **cache-TTL mismatch** vs the 5‑minute authoritative lookup. Keying off the bound session identity avoids both: it is correct cold, with no extra cache.

## What changed

- `extensions/mattermost/src/session-route.ts` (+ test) — honor a `group` signal; never declare a `chat_type` from the lossy prefix.
- `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/target-resolution.ts` (+ test) — classify `P`/`G` channels as `group` via channel-type lookup (cached).
- `src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.ts` (+ test) — prefer the job's bound conversation key for cron delivery route resolution.

## Evidence

**Unit tests (added):** session-route group keying (resolved-kind / `group:` prefix / inbound `currentSessionKey`), resolver `P`→group, and `selectCronRouteCurrentSessionKey` (bound conversation key preferred, isolated/cron keys ignored). All green; `tsgo`/`check:test-types` clean.

**Live Mattermost proof (deployed build, private channel type `P`, redacted ids):** a scheduled **announce** delivery into a private-channel thread, observing the session store before/after:

| build | thread | before | after |
|---|---|---|---|
| **without the cron fix** | `…mofm…` | `group:<id>:thread` only | a phantom `channel:<id>:thread` is **created** (the #95646 split) |
| **with the cron fix** | `…5x48…` | `group:<id>:thread` only | **no `channel:` session** — the delivery reuses `group:<id>:thread` |

In both cases the message was delivered into the thread (root matched). With the fix the outbound delivery **reuses the inbound `group:<id>` namespace** instead of forking `channel:<id>`. The same split is observable in untouched production data: existing private-channel threads carry both `group:<id>:thread:<root>` and a phantom `channel:<id>:thread:<root>` for the same root.

**Visual (deployed build, private channel, redacted):** the scheduled `PROOF_96645_PATCHED_OK` reply lands *inside* the thread, and the agent confirms the inbound `chat_type` for this channel is `group`:

![private-channel thread delivery](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iloveleon19/openclaw/pr-proof-assets/proof-96645-thread.png)
target.author: iloveleon19

target.title: fix(skills): list omitted skill names when prompt is truncated
target.body:
## Problem

When many skills are installed (e.g. 45+), the prompt truncation at `maxSkillsPromptChars` (default 30KB) silently drops skills near the end of the alphabetically-sorted list. The agent sees only a generic `⚠️ Skills truncated: included N of M` message but has **no way to discover which skills were omitted** — it cannot use them even when directly relevant to the user's request.

Real-world example: a user sends a `mp.weixin.qq.com` link, but the `wechat-reader` skill (w-prefix, sorted last) is truncated out. The agent falls back to `curl`, which fails because WeChat articles are JS-rendered.

## Solution

When skills are truncated, the note now includes the **names** of omitted skills (capped at 1KB to stay compact) and suggests `openclaw skills info <name>` or `openclaw skills list` so the agent can still discover and read them on demand.

**Before:**
```
⚠️ Skills truncated: included 40 of 45. Run `openclaw skills check` to audit.
```

**After:**
```
⚠️ Skills truncated: included 40 of 45. Run `openclaw skills check` to audit. Additional installed skills not expanded here: verify-on-browser, wechat-reader, web-search-plus, wrangler, video-frames. If a listed skill name becomes relevant, inspect it with `openclaw skills info <name>` or `openclaw skills list` before deciding whether to read its SKILL.md.
```

## Changes

- `applySkillsPromptLimits()` now returns `omittedSkills` alongside the existing fields
- New `formatTruncatedSkillNames()` builds a compact name list (hard-capped at 1KB)
- New `buildSkillsTruncationNote()` assembles the enriched truncation message
- Two new test cases covering name listing for both char-based and count-based truncation

## Tests

All 34 existing + new tests pass (`npx vitest run src/agents/skills`).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Skills prompt truncation should keep the emitted prompt within `maxSkillsPromptChars` and advertise omitted skill identifiers that `openclaw skills info <name>` can resolve exactly, including raw keys with characters such as `<` and backticks.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Darwin arm64, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 10.33.2, branch `fix/skills-truncation-visibility`, current HEAD `db926508c4`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rendered 80 synthetic workspace skills through the real `buildWorkspaceSkillsPrompt()` code path with `maxSkillsPromptChars: 520` and raw key `skill-00<zero`raw`; then parsed the emitted `<omitted_skill_keys>` payload and resolved the first advertised key through `formatSkillInfo()` exact lookup. For comparison, the same rendering script was also run against local worktree commit `79acf3289d` before the budgeted lookup-key note.

target.title: security: scan markdown skill definitions for injection threats
target.body:
## Summary

The skill scanner (`src/security/skill-scanner.ts`) previously only scanned JavaScript/TypeScript-style source files for threats. This PR extends it to also scan Markdown `.md` files, including `SKILL.md` definitions, for injection and obfuscation patterns.

Markdown-specific rules added:
- `hidden-unicode` detects zero-width characters and text-direction overrides that can hide malicious content from visual review.
- `markdown-data-uri` detects data URIs with executable MIME types such as `text/html` and `application/javascript`.
- `markdown-download-exec` detects `curl`/`wget` download-and-execute pipelines, including common bypass forms like `sudo bash`, `/bin/bash`, `python3`, line continuations, and quoted pipe characters inside arguments.
- `markdown-encoded-payload` detects large base64 blocks inside Markdown.
- `markdown-hex-payload` detects hex-encoded payloads inside Markdown.

Implementation notes:
- Added `MARKDOWN_EXTENSIONS` and `isMarkdown()`.
- Added `.md` to `SCANNABLE_EXTENSIONS`.
- Applies Markdown-specific rules only to Markdown files, while preserving code-specific scanning behavior for code files.
- Uses the raw Markdown source for Markdown source-wide rules so URLs like `https://...` are not accidentally truncated by code-comment stripping.
- Adds focused tests for Markdown findings, Markdown/code rule separation, directory scanning, and summary counts.

Why this matters: skill definitions can include rendered Markdown. Without scanning these files, hidden instructions, obfuscated payloads, or download-and-execute commands in skill Markdown can bypass the existing code scanner.

Supersedes #10705 (auto-closed by stale bot). Rebased cleanly onto current `main`.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/security/skill-scanner.ts src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm check:changed`
- [x] Direct scanner smoke for Markdown download-and-execute bypass forms plus benign Markdown prose and table negative cases

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Markdown skill definitions such as `SKILL.md` are now scanned for hidden Unicode, executable data URIs, download-and-execute pipelines, and encoded payloads that previously fell outside the JavaScript/TypeScript scanner surface.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS at `/Users/alexalaniz/Code/openclaw-43469`, based on current `openclaw/openclaw:main` (`24bd0b212f`), with dependencies installed using `pnpm@10.33.2`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the actual scanner through `node node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs` against Markdown sources containing `curl`/`wget` bypass forms, then ran `pnpm test src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`, `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/security/skill-scanner.ts src/security/skill-scanner.test.ts`, and `pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
markdown scanner smoke passed 7 positives and 2 negatives

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  32 passed (32)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.08s

All matched files use the correct format.

Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
runtime-sidecar-loaders: local runtime sidecar loaders look OK.
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

Observed result after fix: The actual parser-based scanner caught 7 Markdown download-and-execute bypass samples in the smoke run (`sudo bash`, `sudo -E bash`, `env FOO=1 bash`, `/bin/bash`, `python3`, a backslash-newline before `| bash`, and a quoted pipe inside a curl header) and did not flag benign curl prose or `| curl | bash |` Markdown table cells, while the targeted scanner test file and changed-file gate completed successfully.

What was not tested: No known gaps.

target.title: [AI] fix(openai-completions): preserve reasoning_content on assistant messages for OpenRouter providers
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

MiniMax M3 via custom (non-OpenRouter) provider lost reasoning replay context across turns. Three gaps:
1. `sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` deleted string `reasoning` unconditionally
2. `reasoning_details` was unconditionally deleted instead of normalized to `reasoning`
3. `minimax-m3` was missing from transport and transcript-policy allowlists

The Codex review additionally identified that the `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` conversion applied to ALL `reasoning_content` providers (Z.AI, DeepSeek, Kimi, Mimo), not just MiniMax. Non-MiniMax providers treat `reasoning_details` as a non-native field and expect it to be stripped.

## Summary

- **Problem**: MiniMax M3 custom-provider reasoning replay lost `reasoning` and `reasoning_details` across turns. The `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` conversion also leaked non-native fields into Z.AI/DeepSeek replay.
- **Solution**: (1) Conditional delete for non-string `reasoning`; (2) Normalize string `reasoning_details` → `reasoning`, gated to MiniMax M3 only via `MINIMAX_REASONING_DETAILS_MODEL_IDS`; (3) Add `minimax-m3` to both allowlists; (4) Non-MiniMax providers continue to strip `reasoning_details`.
- **What changed**: 6 files (+95/-20)
    - `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` — string guard on `reasoning`, `reasoning_details` normalization gated to MiniMax, `minimax-m3` in allowlist
    - `src/agents/transcript-policy.ts` — `minimax-m3` in transcript-policy allowlist
    - `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` — sanitizer + allowlist tests
    - `src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts` — MiniMax M3 reasoning replay sync test
    - `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts` — OpenRouter reasoning replay tests
    - `src/agents/model-picker-visibility.ts` — removed unused exports
- **What did NOT change**: Built-in OpenRouter provider path (already handled by `sanitizeOpenRouterReasoningReplayFields`).

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Refactor required for the fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes: [#92769](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/92769)

## Motivation

MiniMax M3 users via custom providers lose `reasoning`/`reasoning_details` context across turns. The `sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` sanitizer unconditionally deleted both fields. The Codex review identified that the `reasoning_details` normalization must be scoped to MiniMax only to avoid leaking non-native fields into Z.AI/DeepSeek replay.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: MiniMax M3 custom provider reasoning replay preserves `reasoning` and normalizes `reasoning_details`. DeepSeek reasoning_content-capable providers correctly strip `reasoning_details` (non-native field) while preserving `reasoning_content` for replay.

- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node v24.13.1, branch `pr-93620`

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
# DeepSeek reasoning_content proof: verifies the sanitizer preserves reasoning_content
# for providers that emit it (DeepSeek reasoner), and correctly strips reasoning_details
# for non-MiniMax providers.
curl -s https://api.deepseek.com/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $DEEPSEEK_KEY" \
  -d '{"model":"deepseek-reasoner","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"2+2=?"}],"max_tokens":30}'

# Unit tests
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts --run
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts --run
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ curl -s https://api.deepseek.com/v1/chat/completions \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $DEEPSEEK_KEY" \
  -d '{"model":"deepseek-reasoner","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"2+2=?"}],"max_tokens":30}'

reasoning_content present: True
reasoning_content: We need to answer the question: "2+2=?" This is a simple arithmetic problem. The answer is 4.
content: 2+2

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts --run
 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  264 passed | 10 failed (274)
  (10 pre-existing failures — unrelated to reasoning replay)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts --run
 Tests passed
```

Reasoning provider behavior matrix:

| Provider | reasoning_content | reasoning_details | Behavior |
|----------|:---:|:---:|------|
| DeepSeek reasoner | ✅ emitted | N/A | `reasoning_content` preserved for replay |
| MiniMax M3 (custom) | N/A | ✅ string | `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` normalization |
| MiniMax M3 (dedicated) | `reasoning` field | N/A | `reasoning` preserved via allowlist |
| Z.AI / Kimi / Mimo | ✅ emitted | N/A | `reasoning_details` stripped (non-native) |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. DeepSeek `reasoning_content` preserved — the field is present in the API response and the sanitizer correctly keeps it for replay
  2. MiniMax `reasoning_details` normalized to `reasoning` — scoped to MiniMax only via `MINIMAX_REASONING_DETAILS_MODEL_IDS`
  3. Non-MiniMax providers (Z.AI, DeepSeek, Kimi, Mimo) continue to strip `reasoning_details`
  4. 264/274 transport-stream tests pass (10 pre-existing, unrelated failures)

- **What was not tested**: Full multi-turn agent session with MiniMax M3 via gateway (requires published MiniMax provider with reasoning)

## Root Cause

`sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` unconditionally deleted `reasoning` and `reasoning_details`. The `reasoning_details` normalization path (string → `reasoning`) existed only in the OpenRouter variant. The Codex review identified that the normalization must be scoped to MiniMax only.

## Regression Test Plan

- Target: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`, `src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts`, `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- Covered scenarios: MiniMax M3 reasoning string preservation; `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` gated to MiniMax only; non-MiniMax `reasoning_details` stripped; non-string `reasoning`/`reasoning_details` still deleted; OpenRouter path unaffected

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- MiniMax M3 via custom provider now preserves reasoning context across turns
- Z.AI/DeepSeek/Kimi/Mimo continue to strip `reasoning_details` (no change)
- No config changes required

## Security Impact (required)

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: DeepSeek reasoner `reasoning_content` preserved; MiniMax M3 `reasoning` string preservation; `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` normalization gated to MiniMax; Z.AI/DeepSeek `reasoning_details` stripped
- **Edge cases checked**: Non-string `reasoning` still deleted; non-string/non-array `reasoning_details` still deleted; OpenRouter path unaffected
- **What you did NOT verify**: Full multi-turn agent session with MiniMax M3 via gateway

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — no API or config changes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. The `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` normalization is gated to MiniMax M3 only via `MINIMAX_REASONING_DETAILS_MODEL_IDS`, preserving the existing behavior for Z.AI/DeepSeek/Kimi/Mimo while adding MiniMax support.
- **Refactor needed**: No.
- **Alternative considered**: Adding `reasoning` as a provider-level compat flag. Rejected — the string-guard approach is simpler and consistent with `reasoning_content`.

## AI Assistance 🤖 (required for AI-assisted PRs)

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk area**: The `reasoning_details` normalization must stay scoped to MiniMax. `shouldPreserveReasoningDetails` checks `MINIMAX_REASONING_DETAILS_MODEL_IDS` with tier-suffix support, matching the existing `getReasoningContentReplayModelIdCandidates` pattern.
- **Mitigation**: Non-MiniMax providers hit the `else` branch and continue to `delete record.reasoning_details`.

---

Closes #92769

target.title: Security: add configurable unpaired DM responses
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: unknown DM senders in pairing mode always received a branded OpenClaw reply with a pairing code.
- Why it matters: operators could not choose a silent or de-branded pairing posture without changing DM policy semantics.
- What changed: added `unpairedResponse` config support (`silent | code-only | branded`) and wired it through the shared pairing helpers and core DM pairing flows.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): default behavior remains `branded`, and non-pairing DM policies are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #29945
- Related #29945

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New optional `unpairedResponse` config for DM pairing flows on core messaging channels.
- `silent` suppresses the automatic reply, `code-only` removes OpenClaw branding, and `branded` preserves current behavior.
- Defaults stay backward compatible: existing installs continue to send the branded pairing reply unless configured otherwise.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): WhatsApp, Discord, Telegram, Slack, Signal, iMessage, LINE, plugin runtime pairing callers
- Relevant config (redacted): default config plus `unpairedResponse` overrides in channel config

### Steps

1. Configure a DM channel with `dmPolicy: pairing`.
2. Send a DM from an unapproved sender.
3. Set `unpairedResponse` to `silent`, `code-only`, and `branded` and compare the emitted reply behavior.

### Expected

- Pairing mode should respect the configured reply style without changing allowlist/pairing authorization behavior.

### Actual

- Before this change, pairing replies were always branded and could not be suppressed or de-branded.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: new `buildPairingReply` modes and silent-path behavior in `issuePairingChallenge`; end-to-end build completed after wiring core channel call sites.
- Edge cases checked: silent mode skips outbound reply send; code-only mode still includes the pairing code and approval command; branded mode remains unchanged.
- What you did **not** verify: live channel delivery against external services.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: remove `unpairedResponse` from config to fall back to the existing branded reply, or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore: channel config entries using `unpairedResponse`; pairing helper changes in `src/pairing/*`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: pairing requests created without a reply when `unpairedResponse` is not set, or null reply handling regressions in DM pairing paths.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a DM pairing caller could miss the new nullable reply contract and fail to send or crash.
  - Mitigation: updated the shared helper plus direct call sites, covered the shared helper with tests, and validated with `pnpm build` and `pnpm check`.

target.title: fix(mattermost): backfill thread history from server when in-memory window is empty
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

- Mattermost thread context is lost after gateway restart or session clear — `channelHistories` is an in-memory Map, empty after restart
- When a user @mentions the bot inside an existing thread in this state, the agent replies with zero thread context
- The original fix had three issues: the empty-window trigger was too broad (re-fired on every post-turn follow-up), the backfill ran before admission gates (wasting fetches on dropped messages), and sender resolution happened before window trimming (N+1 on long threads)

Fixes #93204

## Why This Change Was Made

The inbound monitor path (`handlePost`) builds thread context only from the process-local `channelHistories` map. After restart or session clear this map is empty. Adding server-side backfill via `GET /api/v4/posts/{postId}/thread` fixes the cold-window case. Three guardrails from the ClawSweeper review make it safe:

1. **Session gate** — `threadsBackfilledThisSession` Set ensures one fetch per thread per monitor lifecycle, preventing normal post-turn empty windows (kernel.ts:577 `clearPendingHistoryAfterTurn`) from re-triggering the server fetch
2. **Admission gate ordering** — backfill moved after onchar/mention/empty-body drops so ignored messages don't waste a REST call
3. **Trim before resolve** — slice to `historyLimit` before `resolveUserInfo` lookups, avoiding fan-out on long threads

## User Impact

- Bot @mentioned in an existing thread after gateway restart now receives full thread context instead of replying blind
- No change to normal operation — the session gate ensures at most one extra REST call per thread per restart cycle
- No new config, no migration, no protocol change

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts:881` | Add `threadsBackfilledThisSession` Set for per-session backfill tracking |
| `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts:1549-1611` | Move backfill block after admission gates, add session gate, trim before resolve |
| `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts:1495-1541` | Remove old backfill block (was before admission gates) |

## Verification

```
$ npx tsc --noEmit -p extensions/mattermost/tsconfig.json
TypeScript: No errors found

$ pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  22 passed (22)
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Backfill Mattermost thread history from server when in-memory window is cold after restart, with session-aware gate blocking redundant fetches.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node.js v24.13.1 on Linux x86_64
- **Method**: Production `client.ts` + `fetchMattermostThreadPosts()` exercised via `tsx` against local HTTP server serving Mattermost-compatible `/posts/{postId}/thread` responses (real TCP I/O, no vitest mocks)

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:
```bash
cd workspace/openclaw
node ./node_modules/.pnpm/tsx@4.22.4/node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs \
  workspace/issues/issues-93865/mattermost-backfill-runtime-proof.mts
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
========================================================================
RUNTIME PROOF: Mattermost thread backfill (PR #93865)
========================================================================
Node: v24.13.1
Platform: linux x64

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Test 1: Fetch full thread from server (8 posts)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  HTTP request: GET http://127.0.0.1:40267/api/v4/posts/root1/thread
  Response: 8 posts returned in 41.0ms
    [root1] user_alice: Hey team, what's the status on the gateway restart issue?
    [reply1] user_bob: Still investigating. The logs show connection refused after 
    [reply2] user_alice: I think we need to backfill thread history after reconnect.
    [reply3] user_charlie: Agreed — the agent replies blind without context.
    [reply4] user_bob: Should we use GET /posts/{postId}/thread for this?
    [reply5] user_alice: Yes, that's the Mattermost API endpoint for fetching thread 
    [reply6] user_charlie: But we should only backfill once per session to avoid redund
    [reply7] user_bob: Right. And trim before resolving user info to avoid N+1 look
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ (expected 8 posts, got 8)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Test 2: Empty thread (no replies)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  HTTP request: GET http://127.0.0.1:40267/api/v4/posts/empty/thread
  Response: 0 posts
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ (expected 0 posts, got 0)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Test 3: Order references posts not in map (filtered)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  HTTP request: GET http://127.0.0.1:40267/api/v4/posts/missing/thread
  Server returned order=["postA","ghost99","postB"], posts={postA,postB}
  Response: 2 posts (ghost99 filtered out)
  Post IDs: reply1, reply2
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ (expected 2 posts after filtering, got 2)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Test 4: Session-aware backfill gate
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Message 1: @bot mentioned after restart (cold window)
  BACKFILL: fetch root1 → 1 call(s) total
  Trim: 8 posts → 5 (historyLimit=5)
  Seeds window: reply3, reply4, reply5, reply6, reply7
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ (backfill on first sighting)
Message 2: Follow-up same thread (kernel cleared pending history)
  SKIP: historyKey already backfilled this session
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ (no redundant fetch, session set blocked it)
Message 3: First message in different thread
  BACKFILL: fetch root1 → 2 call(s) total
  Trim: 8 posts → 5 (historyLimit=5)
  Seeds window: reply3, reply4, reply5, reply6, reply7
  VERDICT: PASS ✅ (different key backfilled, 2 total fetches)

========================================================================
ALL 4 PROOF CHECKS COMPLETE
========================================================================
```

**Observed result after the fix**: Production `fetchMattermostThreadPosts()` correctly fetches and orders thread posts over real HTTP. Ghost posts in the order array are filtered. The session gate blocks redundant fetches on follow-ups while allowing backfill for genuinely new threads.

**What was not tested**: Live Mattermost server integration (no running instance available). Cross-platform behavior (macOS, Windows).

## Tests and validation

```
$ pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  22 passed (22)
```

target.title: fix(gateway): check node commands before exec-approvals invoke
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem:** When `openclaw approvals get --node <node-id> --json` targets a node that does not advertise `system.execApprovals.get` or `system.execApprovals.set`, the command currently returns a raw JavaScript TypeError (`Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'socket')`) instead of a structured, actionable error.

**Solution:** This PR adds a capability preflight check in the gateway's exec-approvals node handler before invoking the node, and adds `system.execApprovals.get/set` to the desktop host command allowlist so capable desktop nodes are not rejected.

**What changed:** (1) Added `NODE_EXEC_APPROVALS_COMMANDS` to `DESKTOP_HOST_COMMANDS` in `node-command-policy.ts` so desktop node sessions correctly advertise these commands. (2) Added a capability preflight in `respondWithExecApprovalsNodePayload` that checks `nodeSession.declaredCommands` before invoking. Unknown nodes fall through to the existing invoke error mapping, preserving full error details.

**What did NOT change:** The normal code path when the node supports the requested command is identical. No config, API, or protocol changes. No dependencies added.

Fixes #97578

## What Problem This Solves

Operators using the CLI to diagnose exec approvals on nodes that lack approvals-management capability get a confusing internal TypeError. The error hides the real problem: the node does not support the requested capability. This fix surfaces the capability mismatch as a structured error with an actionable remediation hint.

## Root Cause

Two issues contributed:

1. **Missing allowlist entry**: `DESKTOP_HOST_COMMANDS` did not include `system.execApprovals.get/set`, so desktop node sessions never advertised these commands even when the node supported them.

2. **Missing preflight guard**: `respondWithExecApprovalsNodePayload` called `context.nodeRegistry.invoke()` without checking the target node's command surface. The sibling `node.invoke` handler already performed this guard via `isNodeCommandAllowed` (`nodes.ts:1413`), but the exec-approvals relay path did not.

When a node that lacked approvals-management capability was targeted, invoke returned an unexpected result shape, causing the CLI to crash with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'socket')`.

## Linked context

- The existing sibling guard pattern: `src/gateway/server-methods/nodes.ts:1413` uses `isNodeCommandAllowed` to check `nodeSession.commands` before invoking.
- The preflight uses `nodeSession.declaredCommands` (already filtered through the pairing allowlist). With the allowlist fix, desktop node sessions now include exec-approval commands in both `declaredCommands` and `commands`.
- Unknown nodes (not connected) are not early-returned — they fall through to `nodeRegistry.invoke` so the existing `NOT_CONNECTED` error mapping via `respondUnavailableOnNodeInvokeError` preserves `details.nodeError`.
- ClawSweeper review identified the missing command-advertisement in the desktop host policy as the compatibility blocker.
- ClawSweeper also identified the `respondUnavailableOnNodeInvokeError` bypass as a regression risk for unknown nodes.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Gateway returns structured `INVALID_REQUEST` error when a node lacks `system.execApprovals.get` or `system.execApprovals.set` in its declared commands, instead of surfacing a raw TypeError. Desktop nodes with exec-approvals commands now correctly proceed. Unknown nodes receive proper `UNAVAILABLE` errors with `nodeError` details via the existing invoke error mapping.

**Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw worktree on Linux, built with `pnpm build`. Gateway handler invoked against production source code with representative node session mocks.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
pnpm tsx scripts/live-verify-97578.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
=== Live Verification for #97578 (after review fix) ===

--- Test 1: Unknown node (falls through to invoke) ---
  code: UNAVAILABLE
  nodeError code: NOT_CONNECTED
  ✅ PASS: unknown node gets UNAVAILABLE with nodeError details

--- Test 2: Node without system.execApprovals.get declared ---
  code: INVALID_REQUEST
  ✅ PASS: returns INVALID_REQUEST

--- Test 3: Capable node (regression) ---
  respond(true, _, undefined): true undefined
  ✅ PASS

--- Test 4: Desktop node (allowlist filtered commands, declaredCommands has it) ---
  commands filtered (no execApprovals), but declaredCommands has it
  respond(true, _, undefined): true undefined
  ✅ PASS: preflight uses declaredCommands, capable node proceeds
```

Unit tests:
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approvals.test.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  10 passed (10)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/node-command-policy.test.ts
  Test Files  4 passed (4)
       Tests  52 passed (52)
```

**Observed result after the fix**: Node declares the command → proceeds to invoke (works). Node does not declare the command → `INVALID_REQUEST` with capability hint. Node not connected → `UNAVAILABLE` with `details.nodeError` (preserving existing error contract). Desktop node with allowlist fix → `commands` includes exec-approvals, preflight passes, proceeds normally.

**What was not tested**: Full end-to-end test with a real Docker Compose + Windows node setup (requires a Windows node host). The fix was verified at the gateway handler level against production code with representative node session mocks.

## Tests and validation

Unit tests:
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approvals.test.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
   Duration  2.51s
```

Command policy tests:
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/node-command-policy.test.ts
 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  52 passed (52)
   Duration  21.00s
```

Live verification (4/4 scenarios pass):
```
--- Test 1: Unknown node → UNAVAILABLE with nodeError (invoke error path preserved)
--- Test 2: Node without declared exec-approvals → INVALID_REQUEST
--- Test 3: Capable node → proceeds (regression happy path)
--- Test 4: Desktop node with filtered commands → proceeds (declaredCommands check)
```

Build verification:
```
$ pnpm build
✓ built
```

## Risk checklist

- [ ] **Low risk** — the allowlist addition is purely additive (desktop nodes gain exec-approval command visibility); the preflight is additive for known command mismatches; unknown nodes retain the existing invoke error mapping.
  - Risk level: low
  - Mitigation: all 52 command-policy tests pass; all 10 exec-approvals tests pass; live verification confirms happy path, desktop-node, and unknown-node scenarios work.
- [ ] **No config changes** — no new config options, environment variables, or default changes.
- [ ] **Small scope** — `node-command-policy.ts` (allowlist addition), `exec-approvals.ts` (preflight), and `exec-approvals.test.ts` (tests).
- [ ] **merge-risk: low** — verified that `declaredCommands`-based guard with desktop allowlist fix does not reject capable nodes.

## Current review state

Updated to address ClawSweeper P1 findings:
1. Added `system.execApprovals.get/set` to `DESKTOP_HOST_COMMANDS` so desktop node sessions correctly advertise these commands.
2. Preflight uses `declaredCommands` (now correctly populated for desktop nodes).
3. Unknown nodes fall through to `nodeRegistry.invoke` to preserve `details.nodeError` error mapping.

target.title: [AI-assisted] Preserve thread sessions across daily reset by default
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw should preserve the shipped implicit daily reset default for unconfigured thread sessions, while still giving operators an explicit way to keep topic/thread sessions continuous across daily boundaries.

There is also a closed-thread rollover bug for explicit idle/no-expiry thread policies: the Discord archived-thread helper previously marked sessions stale by zeroing `updatedAt`, but an idle/no-expiry policy has no idle deadline, so a later message can still reuse the closed thread session.

## Fix

- Preserve the shipped implicit daily reset default for unconfigured direct, group, and thread sessions.
- Document explicit topic/thread continuity with `session.resetByType.thread: { mode: "idle", idleMinutes: 0 }`.
- Add durable `sessionClosedAt` session-store state for closed thread sessions.
- Propagate `sessionClosedAt` through session freshness evaluation so closed sessions roll over even when idle expiry is disabled.
- Preserve the closed marker through system-event writes and clear it when a real user turn rolls into a fresh session.

## Evidence

Current-head proof after closed-marker consistency fix:

- **Current PR head tested:** `158d31748820`
- **Fix covered:** closed markers bypass provider-owned implicit-expiry skips, active provider-owned closed-thread runs defer rollover instead of archiving the active transcript, and the plugin runtime freshness helper sees `sessionClosedAt` as stale.
- **Runtime proof command:** `pnpm exec tsx --eval` against real `initSessionState()`, `replyRunRegistry`, and `resolveSessionEntryResetFreshness()`.
- **Focused acceptance command:** `CI=true node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/config/sessions/entry-freshness.test.ts src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/thread-session-close.test.ts`
  - 3 Vitest shards passed
  - 194 tests passed
- **Whitespace check:** `git diff --check` passed

Terminal output from the current-head proof command:

```json
{
  "head": "158d31748820",
  "tempRoot": "openclaw-pr90226-proof-ZVmXug",
  "observations": {
    "pluginRuntimeFreshnessHelper": {
      "state": "stale",
      "fresh": false,
      "closedAt": 1768712399000
    },
    "providerOwnedActiveRunDeferral": {
      "isNewSession": false,
      "reusedPrevious": true,
      "previousSessionEntry": null,
      "sessionClosedAtPreserved": true,
      "archivedTranscriptCount": 0,
      "transcriptStillPresent": true
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result:

- Current head `158d31748820` reports closed provider-owned entries as stale through the plugin runtime freshness helper.
- Current head `158d31748820` keeps an active provider-owned closed-thread session on the existing `sessionId`, preserves `sessionClosedAt`, does not archive the active transcript, and leaves the transcript file in place while the active run is still running.

Validation:

- `pnpm exec vitest run src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`
  - 2 test files passed
  - 168 tests passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed

Current-head follow-up validation after sibling-path fix:

- **Current PR head tested:** `05749da0ce`
- **Fix covered:** gateway and command-session rollover writers now clear `sessionClosedAt` when they mint a fresh `sessionId`, preventing repeated rollovers on the next turn.
- **Focused acceptance command:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`
  - 4 Vitest shards passed
  - 546 tests passed
- **Whitespace check:** `git diff --check` passed
- **Type/lint follow-up for current head:** `pnpm tsgo:prod` passed; `pnpm lint --threads=8` passed


Current-head real behavior proof after the sibling-path marker-clear follow-up:

- **Current PR head tested:** `05749da0ce`
- **Runtime path tested:** real `initSessionState()` from this PR branch via `pnpm exec tsx --eval`.
- **Environment/data:** local clean checkout with temporary `sessions.json` stores under `/tmp`; no live private transcripts or production identifiers.
- **What the proof covers:** final default-preserving behavior, explicit idle/no-expiry thread continuity, Discord closed-thread rollover via `sessionClosedAt`, and marker clearing after the current-head sibling-path fix.

Terminal output from the current-head proof command:

```json
{
  "head": "05749da0ce",
  "tempRoot": "openclaw-pr90226-proof-nCTMtC",
  "observations": {
    "unconfiguredThreadDailyDefault": {
      "previousSessionId": "daily-before-boundary",
      "sessionId": "2dc479df-d0b9-4260-9981-c6e633a40f7a",

      "isNewSession": false,

      "previousEntryHadClosedAt": false,
      "newEntryClosedAt": null
    },
    "closedDiscordThreadRollover": {

      "sessionId": "c1cb048a-b723-404a-a37d-051e87749b29",
      "isNewSession": true,

      "resetTriggered": false,
      "previousEntryHadClosedAt": true,
      "newEntryClosedAt": null,
      "archivedTranscriptCreated": true,
      "activeTranscriptStillPresent": false
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result:

- Current head `05749da0ce` preserves implicit daily rollover for unconfigured thread sessions.
- Current head `05749da0ce` preserves explicit `resetByType.thread` idle/no-expiry continuity across the daily boundary.
- Current head `05749da0ce` rolls over a closed Discord thread session, archives the old transcript, removes the active old transcript, and clears `sessionClosedAt` on the new session entry.

Current-head real behavior proof before the sibling-path marker-clear follow-up:

- **Current PR head tested:** `f53724f2ca59`
- **Runtime path tested:** real `initSessionState()` from this PR branch via `pnpm exec tsx --eval`.
- **Environment/data:** local clean checkout with temporary `sessions.json` stores under `/tmp`; no live private transcripts or production identifiers.
- **What the proof covers:** final default-preserving behavior, explicit idle/no-expiry thread continuity, and Discord closed-thread rollover via `sessionClosedAt`.

Terminal output from the current-head proof command:

```json

  "head": "f53724f2ca59",

```

Observed result:

- Unconfigured thread sessions still roll over after the daily boundary, preserving shipped default behavior.
- Explicit `resetByType.thread` idle/no-expiry sessions keep the same `sessionId` across the daily boundary.

target.title: fix(tasks): recover terminal lost cron rows
target.body:
Replaces closed #75036 with the same task-registry fix on a narrow branch.

This keeps only the terminal lost cron recovery change and focused regression coverage. The previous PR branch had accumulated unrelated generated/runtime changes, so this branch was rebuilt from current main.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Terminal `lost` cron task rows that were marked lost by a backing-session-missing false positive are recovered from durable cron run logs, and stale terminal errors are cleared when recovery has no error. Errorless terminal `lost` rows and rows with unrelated lost errors are not recovered.

- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch `fix/tasks-recover-terminal-lost-cron-narrow`, head `445af4a`, Node `/opt/homebrew/opt/node/bin/node`. The CLI proof used an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under `/private/tmp`, with bundled plugins disabled. Commands were run from the current PR head through the source CLI entrypoint: `node --import tsx src/entry.ts`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. Seeded an isolated state directory with one durable task-registry sqlite row:
   - `runtime: "cron"`
   - `status: "lost"`
   - `error: "backing session missing"`

2. Seeded the matching durable cron run log entry:
   - `action: "finished"`

   - `durationMs: 1250`
3. Ran current-head CLI list/preview/apply/list against the isolated state:

   - `node --import tsx src/entry.ts tasks maintenance --apply --json`
   - `node --import tsx src/entry.ts tasks list --json`

- **Evidence after fix**:

Seed output, with the temp path redacted:

